tactical_shape: Tuchel wants speed, physicality, risk-taking and running power
standout_players: Declan Rice 7/10 — back to his marauding best early on
Reece James 6/10 — bright start before being clattered and needing lengthy treatment
Marc Guéhi 6/10 — range of passing adds so much
Noni Madueke 6/10 — directness caused Ghana problems
Jude Bellingham 6/10 — effort could not be faulted but lacked cutting edge
Harry Kane 6/10 — only eight touches in a quiet first half; should have buried a late rebound
Bukayo Saka 7/10 — injected urgency after coming on
Nico O'Reilly 6/10 — unlucky not to score with a header
Eberechi Eze 5/10 — looked far more comfortable further forward
fatigue_injuries: Reece James needed lengthy treatment after being clattered by Jordan Ayew
home_read.momentum: England were held to a frustrating 0-0, with Tuchel still driving an energetic, punchy identity and hopes of a stronger connection with fans after the Croatia win.

tactical_shape: defended well and made England struggle to find a way through
compact midfield protection with strong defensive work
standout_players: Benjamin Asare 6/10 — did not have much to do
Jerome Opoku 8/10 — a giant at the back who England could not play through
Jonas Adjetey 8/10 — organiser in chief of the defence; looked a world beater
Caleb Yirenkyi 7/10 — another impressive showing in midfield
Thomas Partey 7/10 — marshalled Ghana’s midfield superbly and limited Kane's influence
Kwasi Sibo 7/10 — ran his socks off and made a crucial block
Antoine Semenyo 7/10 — threat England were wary of
Jordan Ayew 7/10 — put himself about as usual
Iñaki Williams 6/10 — did his defensive duties
Asare's first Ghana league goalkeeper at a World Cup
away_read.momentum: A disciplined, physical display earned Ghana a valuable point, with their defensive organisation and midfield work suggesting growing confidence.
